programs.name: Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky (PACE)

eligibility.income_limits: No specific income limits for PACE enrollment; eligibility not based on financial criteria. Medicaid eligibility (which covers part of premium) depends on separate KY Medicaid income/asset limits, which PACE staff can assess[1][3][5][6].
eligibility.asset_limits: No asset limits for PACE enrollment itself. Medicaid portion requires meeting KY Medicaid asset rules (e.g., typically $2,000 for individual), assessed by PACE staff[6][7].
other_requirements: Live in the Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky service area
Certified by Kentucky as meeting nursing home level of care (per 907 KAR 1:022 or 907 KAR 3:250)
Able to live safely in the community at time of enrollment with PACE services
Have Medicare, Medicaid, or ability to privately pay monthly premium

benefits.value: All-inclusive: primary/specialty medical care, prescription medications, adult day services, home care, mental health/social services, skilled therapies, nursing home care, hospital care; coordinated by interdisciplinary team; covers all preventive, primary, acute, long-term needs[1][6][7].

methods: In-person: Visit PACE Center (locations in Services section of seniorcommunitycareky.org)[1][6]
Contact for intake, clinical assessment at center, custom care plan development, enrollment paperwork completed by team[1]
application.processing_time: Not specified; involves intake, clinical assessment, care plan review, paperwork[1].
forms: Enrollment paperwork completed by PACE team after assessment; no specific form name/number listed[1]
documents: Diagnosis and health information (helpful to have ready)[8]

geography.restrictions: Limited to Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky service area (specific counties not listed in sources; check with provider). Other PACE providers serve different KY areas, e.g., Bullitt/Grayson/Hardin/Hart/LaRue/Nelson[2]; Northern KY separate[7].
geography.regional_variations: Multiple PACE providers in KY with different service areas, phone numbers, centers (e.g., Senior CommUnity Care of KY vs. Northern KY)[1][2][6][7].
offices_or_providers: Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky PACE (central KY): seniorcommunitycareky.org, (502) 314-2059[1][6]
Senior CommUnity Care of Northern Kentucky: (859) 970-2450[7]

gotchas: Must live in specific PACE provider's service area; not statewide—confirm counties with provider[1][2][6]
Nursing home level of care certification required by KY (not automatic; assessed)[1][3][4]
Private pay option if no Medicaid (covers Medicaid's premium share; costs vary)[3][6]
Cannot be in Medicare Advantage, hospice, or certain other programs[5]
Voluntary; can dis-enroll anytime via social worker[1]
programs.data_shape_notes: County-restricted to provider service areas (not statewide); no direct income/asset test for enrollment (Medicaid separate); requires state nursing home certification; multiple regional PACE providers in KY with own contacts

programs.name: Senior CommUnity Care of Northern Kentucky (PACE)

eligibility.income_limits: No specific dollar amounts or household size table provided; eligibility tied to Medicaid income limits. If eligible for Medicaid, services are free; if over limits, pay premium or Patient Monthly Liability. PACE staff assist with Medicaid determination.[1][3]
eligibility.asset_limits: Tied to Medicaid asset limits (specific amounts not detailed); PACE staff help determine eligibility.[3][4]
other_requirements: Live in the Senior CommUnity Care of Northern Kentucky service area (Northern Kentucky region)
Meet nursing home level of care (as defined in 907 KAR 1:022)
Be able to live safely in the community with program services[1][2][3]

benefits.value: Primary and specialty medical care, prescription medications, adult day services, home care services, mental health and social services, skilled therapies, nursing home care, hospital care, rehabilitation, transportation, day center, social activities, meal assistance—all coordinated by interdisciplinary team with custom care plan. No specific dollar amounts or hours stated.[3][4][7]

methods: In-person: Visit PACE Center at 47 Cavalier Blvd, Ste 140, Florence, KY 41042[3][8]
application.processing_time: Not specified; process includes intake, clinical assessment at center, custom care plan development, enrollment paperwork[1]
forms: Enrollment paperwork completed by team after assessment (no specific form name/number listed)
documents: Not explicitly listed; prepare for intake assessment and clinical evaluation with doctor, nurses, interdisciplinary team[1]

geography.restrictions: Northern Kentucky service area only (specific counties not detailed; check PACE coverage map via state resources)[1][2][6]
geography.regional_variations: Multiple PACE providers in Kentucky with different service areas, enrollment contacts, and centers (e.g., Northern KY vs. other regions); wait times may vary but not specified[2][4][6]

gotchas: Must follow custom care plan; liable for unauthorized/out-of-program services (except emergencies)[1]
Enrollment voluntary; can dis-enroll anytime via social worker[1]
Paid by Medicare/Medicaid or private premium if over Medicaid limits—staff handles claims but confirm personal liability[1][3]
Service area restricted to Northern Kentucky; not statewide[1][6]
Requires nursing home level of care certification but ability to live safely in community with services[2][3]
programs.data_shape_notes: Restricted to Northern Kentucky service area with one main center; no fixed income/asset dollar tables (tied to Medicaid); comprehensive in-kind services via interdisciplinary team, no tiers or hours specified

programs.name: Senior Companion Program (Community Action of Southern Kentucky)
programs._raw:
I cannot provide the comprehensive JSON guide you've requested because the search results do not contain sufficient specific information about the Community Action of Southern Kentucky's Senior Companion Program.

Here's what the search results reveal and what's missing:

## What I Can Confirm

**Eligibility (Partial):**
- Age: 55 or older[1][3]
- Income limit: 200% of federal poverty level[2]
- Must pass background check, driver's history check, fingerprinting, and NSOR check[2]
- Must pass a road test evaluation[2]

**Services (Partial):**
- One-on-one companionship, typically 1-2 times per week[2]
- Light housework, errands, transportation, escort to medical appointments[3][5]
- Volunteers receive a $4.00 hourly stipend[2]
- Minimum 5 hours per week service requirement (up to 40 hours)[2]

**Application Contact:**
- Phone: 270-692-2135[3]
- Email: mary.blake@ckcac.org[3]

**Processing Time:**
- Background check process: 6-8 weeks[2]
- Training requirement: 20+ hours of onboarding[2]

## Critical Gaps

The search results do **not** contain:
- Specific dollar amounts for income limits by household size
- Asset limits or exemptions
- Exact application procedures or forms
- Official website URL for Community Action of Southern Kentucky
- Waitlist information or current wait times
- Regional variations within Kentucky
- How this program differs from other Senior Companion programs
- Whether the program currently has openings or is accepting applications

**Recommendation:** Contact the program directly at 270-692-2135 or mary.blake@ckcac.org[3] to obtain the detailed, program-specific information needed for your comprehensive guide. They can provide income tables, current processing times, and application procedures specific to their service area.
